text: I want to show you the projects I've been working on the last couple of weeks here.
I'm redoing the boys furniture in their room.
I was a little stupid in trying to do three projects at one time because it was a little
bit more than I, it bit off a little bit more than I could chew I think.
But I wanted to talk about what I'm doing with this.
I'm doing a dry brush technique which is really easy if you want to make furniture look older
and used and a little bit more primitive.
Plus you don't have to cover up all the flaws in the furniture.
First I had to strip and sand all the furniture because it was painted an off white color
which was a real pain in the neck and I will never do that again.
But I didn't want the off white showing through to the furniture.
I wanted the wood to show through because this is for the boys room and I've got the
white.
I just want to be masculine enough.
So you can see that first after I sanded it or stripped it and sanded it then I had to
cover it with the layer that I wanted to show through the top layer which was a light brown.
This technique is really easy because again you don't have to cover up any imperfections
and the point is that you want the stuff at the bottom showing through.
So what you do is you get your brush and you get it pretty dry and you just go in short
strokes back and forth.
Actually no one has ever shown me this before so I have no idea if I'm doing it right.
You go in short strokes back and forth over the furniture piece that you're painting and
what happens is you get this nice 3D effect of many layers showing through.
It looks old and used and gives character to the piece that you're going to have in
your room.
So you're going to have a nice 3D effect.
So you're going to have a nice 3D effect.
Instead of just a brand new shellac piece that you can buy at a furniture store.
So I'm not sure if I'm going to do another layer of orange.
I think I might want it to be a little bit darker but I still want the wood to show through
so I'm debating on whether I'll do a second coat of orange over this brown and then after
you do that we'll sand it down around the edges and cross the front a little bit and
hopefully we'll have some nice pieces in the boys room with a lot of character and not
a lot of work.
Well a lot of work for the sanding and stripping.
I would not recommend that.
But this part is easy.
